kept_artifacts.title: GridCore Manufacturing breaks ground on new power transformer production plant
kept_artifacts.subheadline: Expansion is intended to increase domestic output for utility and grid modernization projects.
kept_artifacts.body_text: GridCore Manufacturing today announced that it has broken ground on a new power transformer production plant in Oklahoma. The facility is expected to increase output for utility customers pursuing grid modernization, substation replacement, and transmission buildout. The company said the new site will add significant annual manufacturing capacity once fully operational.

_prefilter.reason: Strong-field event-form hit in an explicit capital-flow family.

kept_artifacts.title: ThermaLoop Systems signs multi-year supply agreement for data center cooling modules
kept_artifacts.subheadline: Agreement supports accelerated shipments for AI infrastructure deployments.
kept_artifacts.body_text: ThermaLoop Systems announced a multi-year supply agreement covering liquid cooling modules and distribution units for large AI data center deployments. The company said the agreement supports a planned production increase at its existing thermal management operations.

_prefilter.reason: Strong-field event-form hit in an explicit capital-flow family.

kept_artifacts.title: PrimeForge Power to expand generator package factory for data center and utility resilience demand
kept_artifacts.subheadline: Company cites backup power demand from digital infrastructure and utility customers.
kept_artifacts.body_text: PrimeForge Power announced an expansion of its generator package factory to support rising demand from data center operators and utility resilience programs. The company said the project will add new production capacity for backup-power systems, switchboards and related balance-of-system equipment.

_prefilter.reason: Strong-field event-form hit in an explicit capital-flow family.

kept_artifacts.title: Lamina Grid Products adds core and coil line to support transformer backlog
kept_artifacts.subheadline: Expansion targets utility and data center transformer demand.
kept_artifacts.body_text: Lamina Grid Products announced a new core and coil manufacturing line intended to support rising transformer backlog and utility procurement activity. The company said the expansion will supply domestic transformer OEMs serving substation, data center and industrial power projects.

_prefilter.reason: Strong-field event-form hit in an explicit capital-flow family.

kept_artifacts.title: MotionArc Controls enters joint venture for industrial automation systems
kept_artifacts.subheadline: JV will expand product development, assembly and go-to-market efforts.
kept_artifacts.body_text: MotionArc Controls announced a joint venture focused on industrial automation systems. The company said the partnership will support expanded product development, assembly and marketing for factory automation offerings.

kept_artifacts.title: MeterWave Technologies expands smart meter production lines for utility modernization programs
kept_artifacts.subheadline: Project supports grid modernization and utility rollout schedules.
kept_artifacts.body_text: MeterWave Technologies announced an expansion of its smart meter production lines to support utility modernization programs and advanced metering deployments. The company said the project increases output capacity and shortens lead times for utility customers.

kept_artifacts.title: ConductorWorks invests in new medium-voltage cable factory for utility and data center projects
kept_artifacts.subheadline: Facility will increase output for grid upgrades and large-load connections.
kept_artifacts.body_text: ConductorWorks announced an investment in a new medium-voltage cable factory designed to support utility grid upgrades, substation work and large-load connection projects. The company said the site will add domestic output capacity as project demand increases.

kept_artifacts.title: MacroFab Holdings announces $600 million U.S. industrial expansion plan
kept_artifacts.subheadline: Capital program spans multiple facilities and business lines.
kept_artifacts.body_text: MacroFab Holdings announced a $600 million U.S. industrial expansion plan spanning multiple facilities and business lines. The company described the move as a broad domestic manufacturing growth initiative without tying it to one specific physical system.

kept_artifacts.title: NorthRiver Digital Infrastructure breaks ground on second hyperscale data center campus phase
kept_artifacts.subheadline: New phase adds power-delivery, cooling and shell capacity.
kept_artifacts.body_text: NorthRiver Digital Infrastructure announced groundbreaking for the second phase of its hyperscale data center campus. The company said the project adds new shell space, cooling infrastructure, and power-delivery systems as it expands campus capacity.

kept_artifacts.title: Summit Compute Parks advances 250MW data center campus construction

kept_artifacts.body_text: Summit Compute Parks announced continued construction of a 250MW data center campus. The company said the project includes dedicated substations, on-site power infrastructure, and supporting cooling systems to bring new campus capacity online.

kept_artifacts.title: PeakSpan Power Systems expands generator module assembly for utility and data center demand

kept_artifacts.body_text: PeakSpan Power Systems announced an expansion of its generator module assembly operations to serve utility response projects and data center backup-power demand. The company said the expansion increases package throughput for gas and dual-fuel generator systems.

kept_artifacts.title: FlexGen Turbine Packages invests in new manufacturing line for fast-start power equipment
kept_artifacts.subheadline: Project supports utility balancing needs and digital infrastructure resilience.
kept_artifacts.body_text: FlexGen Turbine Packages announced an investment in a new manufacturing line for fast-start power equipment and associated balance-of-plant systems. The company said the line will support utility balancing needs and resilience demand from digital infrastructure operators.

kept_artifacts.title: Schneider Electric to invest $700M in US manufacturing
kept_artifacts.body_text: Schneider Electric plans to invest $700 million in its U.S. operations through 2027. The money will fund facility upgrades, expansions and openings across eight sites to meet rising demand from data centers and electricity infrastructure.

kept_artifacts.title: GE Vernova to invest nearly $600M in US factories

kept_artifacts.title: Rolls-Royce invests $75M in South Carolina engine plant
kept_artifacts.body_text: Rolls-Royce will invest $75 million to expand its Aiken, South Carolina, engine plant to meet surging data center demand. The 60,000-square-foot expansion will increase production of large engines used to power data centers and industrial facilities.

kept_artifacts.title: Rockwell Automation confirms Wisconsin factory location, part of $2B US expansion

kept_artifacts.title: Roche to expand, open Indianapolis and North Carolina sites

kept_artifacts.title: EdgeCore expands Mesa data center campus in Arizona

kept_artifacts.title: EdgeCore begins construction at 114MW Northern Virginia campus

kept_artifacts.title: EdgeCore secures $235 million in ABS financing

kept_artifacts.title: As load grows, Southern raises spending plan to $81B

review_artifacts.title: Power enclosure maker AVL to establish its first US plant

review_artifacts.title: Hyundai boosts US investments to $26B through 2028

review_artifacts.title: PG&E data center pipeline swells to 10GW
review_artifacts.body_text: PG&E reported 10GW of data center capacity in its pipeline, with 17 projects totaling 1.5GW in final engineering phases and operations expected to begin between 2026 and 2030.

review_artifacts.title: US utility Exelon reports data center pipeline of 33GW
review_artifacts.body_text: Exelon said it has 33GW of data centers interested in connecting to its system, with 17GW already in line to connect. The utility is considering options for building and owning generation to meet the demand.

review_artifacts.title: First Energy data center pipeline surges to 2.6GW by 2029
review_artifacts.body_text: First Energy reported that its pipeline of potential data centers surged to 2.6GW by 2029, almost double the prior reported pipeline. The utility is considering delaying coal retirements or adding gas-fired generation to serve the load.

review_artifacts.title: PPL Electric's data center pipeline soars to 14GW
review_artifacts.body_text: PPL Electric said its advanced-stage data center interconnection pipeline grew to 14GW. The utility is taking a two-pronged approach to support data center growth and expects load to ramp sharply over the decade.

review_artifacts.title: Hitachi Energy commits $250M to address transformer shortage
review_artifacts.body_text: Hitachi Energy announced a $250 million investment to expand global production of transformer components, with more than 40% dedicated to the U.S. The company said data center development has pushed transformer demand beyond initial projections.

review_artifacts.title: DTE inks first data center deal to grow electric load 25%
review_artifacts.body_text: DTE signed a 1.4GW agreement to serve a hyperscale data center and sees another 7GW of potential large loads. The utility now has a $30 billion investment pipeline for generation, distribution and other infrastructure from 2026 to 2030.

review_artifacts.title: Exelon data center pipeline jumps to 17 GW as load forecast turns positive
review_artifacts.body_text: Exelon utilities' pipeline of data centers and other high-density load projects more than doubled to 17GW. The company expects positive load growth and called for new approaches to ensure adequate power supplies.

review_artifacts.title: FirstEnergy’s 5-year data center pipeline doubles to 3 GW
review_artifacts.body_text: FirstEnergy's pipeline of potential data centers nearly doubled to almost 3GW by 2029. The utility expects industrial sales including data centers to grow 5.1% annually over the next five years.

review_artifacts.title: FirstEnergy expects peak load to grow 45% by 2035 on data centers
review_artifacts.body_text: FirstEnergy expects system peak load to jump 45% by 2035, driven by data center development. The company said developers will be responsible for incremental investments needed to support that growth.

review_artifacts.title: Italy-based Westrafo to build its first US transformer plant
